stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
An aircraft is re-positioning from Europe to Canada, and it is planned to enter the NAT HLA at DOGAL and follow westbound track G which begins there. 20 minutes before reaching DOGAL, one of the aircraft's two FMSs fails, and cannot be reset. Which of the following must the pilots do?

options.a: Advise ATC of the failure and continue the flight as planned but with increased monitoring due to lack of redundancy.
options.b: Remain outside NAT HLA unless ATC approves a reroute via GOMUP-KFV-OZN and onwards.
options.c: Request a random route outside of the OTS since flight along the OTS requires 2 serviceable LRNSs, but a random route only requires 1.
options.d: Request a flight level that is either above or below the NAT HLA, but track G may still be followed.
